title: J. Jonah Jameson
content:
{{CharHub|J. Jonah Jameson|J. Jonah Jameson}} {{Character |image = J. Jonah Jameson.png |real name = J{{Conjecture|ohn}} Jonah Jameson, Jr. |alias = {{Alias |primary = J. Jonah Jameson{{Ref|SMFFH}}}} |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA}} |gender = Male |affiliation = ''[[The Daily Bugle]]'' |movie = ''[[Spider-Man: Far From Home]]'' ([[List of Post-credits Scenes|mid-credits scene]])
''[[Spider-Man: No Way Home]]'' |web series = ''[[The Daily Bugle/Promotional Campaign|The Daily Bugle]]'' |actor = [[J.K. Simmons]] |status = Alive}} {{Quote|We here at ''[[The Daily Bugle]]'' will not rest until we've uncovered the truth behind [[Spider-Man|his]] web of lies. I'm J. Jonah Jameson, good night, and God help us all.|J. Jonah Jameson|The Daily Bugle/Promotional Campaign}} '''J{{Conjecture|ohn}} "J" Jonah Jameson, Jr.''' is the show host of ''[[The Daily Bugle]]'', who mainly focuses on reporting on the actions of heroes and vigilantes, including [[Mysterio]], whom he reveres as a great hero, and [[Spider-Man]], whom he views as a menace to society. He, along with Mysterio and [[William Ginter Riva]], is responsible for exposing Spider-Man's true identity of Peter Parker to the public. The reveal gave ''The Daily Bugle'' a surge in popularity, and Jameson continued to report on Peter Parker, fueling public hatred of the hero, and support for the late Mysterio. Tipped off that Parker was taking refuge at [[Happy Hogan's Condominium]], Jameson traveled there in hopes of getting incriminating footage, only to witness the clash between Spider-Man and the various villains that had been unleashed from the [[Multiverse]]. Jameson was forced to flee the scene when the battle got out of hand, and later was called by Parker himself, who told the world he would make right his mistakes. Jameson alongside everyone else in the world would forget Peter Parker as a result of [[Doctor Strange]]'s spell, although this did not stop Jameson from continuing his crusade against Spider-Man. ==Biography== ===Early Life=== ====Educated at Home==== {{Quote|Might be a good time for homeschool, folks; worked wonders for yours truly.|J. Jonah Jameson|The Daily Bugle/Promotional Campaign}} Prior to adulthood, J. Jonah Jameson was homeschooled. This experience would inform his future advocacy against the public school system.{{Ref|TDBPCWS}} ===''[[The Daily Bugle]]''=== ====Reporting on Spider-Man==== {{Quote|Governments around the world launched investigations into the murderer known as [[Spider-Man]], aka Peter Parker, aka the web-headed war criminal, who for years has been terrorizing the decent citizens of [[New York City|New York]]. Well now, this city and the [[Earth|world]] see him for what he truly is.|J. Jonah Jameson|Spider-Man: No Way Home}} [[File:JJonahJameson-SpiderMenace1.png|{{AltFloat}}|Jameson calls [[Spider-Man]] an evil psychopath]] In [[2024]], while reporting from ''[[The Daily Bugle]]'', Jameson had witnessed the actions of [[Spider-Man]] during his [[Mysterio's Masquerade|battles]] against the [[Elementals]] in [[Venice]] and [[Prague]], where he had become overwhelmed and inadvertently caused some massive destruction.{{Ref|SMFFH}} Giving his report of the incidents on his show, Jameson called Spider-Man a psychopath who was wielding powers that he did not deserve. Jameson had also called [[Mysterio]], who had seemingly destroyed [[Hydro-Man]] and [[Molten Man]], a true hero. Upon finishing his report on Spider-Man, Jameson thanked his audience for watching and recommended that they like and subscribe. In the following days, Jameson posted articles accusing Spider-Man from [[Attack on the Manfredi Crime Family|taking down]] the [[Manfredi Crime Family]] under orders of one of their rivals to worsen the [[Mafia Cold War]] on [[New York City]], discrediting the claims of [[Ned Leeds]] and [[Flash Thompson]] in regards to their experiences on [[Europe]] and even commenting about a failed raid on [[Area 51]], which he compared to the past controversy surrounding purchasable [[Chitauri]] weapons in the black market in the aftermath of the [[Battle of New York]] back in [[2012]].{{Ref|TDBPCWS}} [[File:The Daily Bugle (FFH).png|{{AltFloat}}|Jameson announcing [[Spider-Man]]'s identity]] Jameson later received edited footage from [[William Ginter Riva]] of a [[Battle of London|battle]] between Spider-Man and Mysterio in [[London]]. During the battle, Mysterio filmed himself claiming that he had intentions to save them from Spider-Man and the [[Stark Industries Combat Drones]], framing Spider-Man for murdering him. Having watched the footage and believing Mysterio's claims, Jameson proceeded to expose Spider-Man's real identity as [[Peter Parker]]. [[File:J. Jonah Jameson (NWH).png|{{AltFloat}}|Jameson continues to expose [[Peter Parker]]]] Jameson aired the footage on ''The Daily Bugle'' after sharing it with [[Pat Kiernan]] from ''[[NY1]]'', as he also hailed Mysterio as the greatest superhero of all time and calling the exposé of Parker the real blockbuster.{{Ref|TDBPCWS}} In the wake of this last report, Jameson and ''The Daily Bugle'' reached out to some teachers of the [[Midtown School of Science and Technology]] for more background on Parker, but they declined to comment. Jameson then bashed Parker by calling him names and exposed his friends, [[Ned Leeds]] and [[Michelle Jones]] as well. He then broadcasted a video of a Mysterio supporter splashing green paint on Parker.{{Ref|SMNWH}}{{Ref|SMNWHTMFSV}} ====Further Reports==== {{Quote|[[Spider-Man]]: a psychopath wielding powers he has no right to possess! Trying to pass himself off as a hero! Well, let me tell you something, kid, you're no hero! [[Mysterio]], that's a hero. You are a criminal, a miscreant, a masked marauder, a menace!|J. Jonah Jameson|The Daily Bugle/Promotional Campaign}} {{Expand|TDBPC on NWH; Web of Lies, Conversation with Burke, etc.}} Sometime after [[Spider-Man]]'s identity was revealed, Jameson hired [[Betty Brant]] as an intern to manage the Bugle's TikTok account.{{Ref|TDBPCTT1}} Jameson occasionally featured at the end of the videos to comment on the topic of Brant's report or correct her if she spoke too highly of Spider-Man.{{Ref|TDBPCTT2}}{{Ref|TDBPCTT7}}{{Ref|TDBPCTT8}} ====Finding Spider-Man==== [[File:Why Does Spider-Man Hate National Landmarks.png|{{AltFloat}}|Jameson promotes his own [[Daily Bugle Supplements|supplements]]]] {{Quote|When will people wake up and realize that everywhere [[Spider-Man]] goes, chaos and calamity ensue. Everything Spider-Man touches comes to ruin. And we, the innocents, are left to pick up the pieces.|J. Jonah Jameson|Spider-Man: No Way Home}} Jameson was soon alerted by a ''The Daily Bugle'' truck driver that he had found [[Spider-Man]] in a [[F.E.A.S.T.]] truck, sending [[Norman Osborn]] to the [[New York Sanctum]] and spoken to by [[May Parker]]. When Jameson realized Spider-Man was no longer there, he was enraged at him. [[File:JJJDinosaur.png|{{AltFloat}}|Jameson witnesses [[Lizard]] running free]] Soon after, Jameson and his crew got a tip and went to [[Happy Hogan]]'s [[Happy Hogan's Condominium|condominium]] where Parker was inside. Jameson waited impatiently outside as he informed [[Damage Control]] of his suspicion of Spider-Man. As they waited, he witnessed [[Doctor Octopus]] being shot out of a window by bursts of electricity. Jameson, recognizing him from the [[Alexander Hamilton Bridge]], told his cameraman to catch him as he jumped onto another building. He was then startled when [[Lizard]] tore out of the F.E.A.S.T. truck and ran away. [[File:JJJ NWH.png|{{AltFloat}}|Jameson watches as [[Spider-Man]] escapes]] Soon after, they witnessed [[Sandman|sand]] swirl up around them, and blackout occurred within the condominium. Looking up once more, he spotted Parker engaged in a fight against Lizard and smiled that he had found him. However, once the condominium began to collapse due to the [[Escape from Spider-Man|fight]] inside and the release of [[Pumpkin Bombs]] from [[Green Goblin]], he and his crew were forced to escape. [[File:J. Jonah Jameson (No Way Home).png|{{AltFloat}}|Jameson gives his report about the battle]] Shortly after, Jameson and his crew returned to the now destroyed condominium complex and reported on the aftermath of the battle that had taken place. Jameson informed his viewers that Spider-Man was responsible for all the damage and destruction, as well for the tragedy of May's death. He also stated that chaos and calamity ensued wherever Spider-Man went and that everything he touched came to ruin, leaving it upon the people to pick up the pieces. He then bid his viewers good night on a somber tone.{{Ref|SMNWH}} ====Speaking with Peter Parker==== [[File:JJJ On the Line 2.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Jameson speaks with [[Peter Parker]]]] {{Quote|So, Peter Parker. What pernicious propaganda are you peddling?"
"Just the truth. Truth is... that this is all my fault. I accidentally brought those dangerous people here."
"Wow. He admits it!|J. Jonah Jameson and [[Spider-Man]]|Spider-Man: No Way Home}} Later, Jameson received a call from Parker via a video broadcast, revealing the truth that he had brought the enhanced perpetrators to their city. Jameson relished as that Parker admitted to being responsible, before he revealed his location at the [[Statue of Liberty]]. Jameson then told the audience that Parker would destroy another national monument.{{Ref|SMNWH}} ====Forgetting Spider-Man's Identity==== [[File:Only a Coward Hides His True Intentions.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Jameson questions when [[Spider-Man]] will reveal his identity]] {{Quote|It's been a few weeks since the [[Battle at Liberty Island|fiasco]] on the [[Statue of Liberty]] and [[Spider-Man]]'s cultists continue to contend that the vile vigilante is a hero. Well, if he were a hero, he'd unmask himself and tell us who he really is. Because only a coward conceals his identity.|J. Jonah Jameson|Spider-Man: No Way Home}} Hours later, Jameson, along with the rest of the world, had his memory of [[Peter Parker]] erased. In December, Jameson operated at his [[Daily Bugle Broadcasting Center|studio]], continuing to question when Spider-Man would make his identity be known, saying that if he was truly a hero, he wouldn't be hiding behind the mask like a coward.{{Ref|SMNWH}} ==Abilities== {{Quote|Rest assured, ladies and gentlemen, this reporter will uncover those intentions, come hell or high water.|J. Jonah Jameson|Spider-Man: No Way Home}} *'''Expert Investigator''': Jameson is a skilled investigator, able to find facts about stories and twist words to fit the way he reports the news. Although he often reports with sensationalism in mind, he tells stories based on facts and interviews, and inserts his own commentary regarding the story being told. He uses his stories to further his own agenda, such as when he reported on a failed raid of [[Area 51]] and managed to criticize [[Avengers|superheroes]] for allowing [[Chitauri]] weapons to be sold on the streets after the [[Battle of New York]]. ==Facilities== *'''[[Daily Bugle Broadcasting Center]]''': Jameson operated ''[[The Daily Bugle]]'' out of its broadcasting center, where he filmed many of his reports. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *Joan Jameson - Wife ===Allies=== *[[The Daily Bugle]] **[[Betty Brant]] - Intern *''[[NY1]]'' **[[Pat Kiernan]] *[[Mysterio|Quentin Beck/Mysterio]] † - Idol *[[William Ginter Riva]] - Contact *[[Flash Thompson]] - Interviewee *[[Andre Wilson]] - Interviewee *[[Jack Triconi]] - Interviewee *[[Roger Harrington]] - Interviewee *[[Isaiah Sorenson]] - Interviewee *[[David Jerome]] - Interviewee *[[Cammie Conroy]] - Interviewee *[[Karen Oggerton]] - Interviewee *[[Elijah Cortez]] - Interviewee *[[Walter Burke]] - Interviewee *[[Graham Dijoni]] - Interviewee *[[Ed King]] - Interviewee *[[New York City Police Department]] *[[United States Department of Damage Control]] {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Spider-Man|Peter Parker/Spider-Man]] *[[Ned Leeds]] *[[Michelle Jones]] *[[Lizard|Curt Connors/Lizard]] *[[Doctor Octopus|Otto Octavius/Doctor Octopus]] *[[Electro|Max Dillon/Electro]] *[[Sandman|Flint Marko/Sandman]] *[[Green Goblin|Norman Osborn/Green Goblin]] {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== {| class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wikitable" width="100%" ! Appearances for J. Jonah Jameson |- | {{Scroll box|content= In chronological order: *{{Appearance|SMFFH}} *{{Appearance|SMNWH}} **{{Appearance|TDBPCYT3}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCYT1}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCYT4}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCYT2}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCYT5}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCTT12}} (mentioned; concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCTT2}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCTT6}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCTT7}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCTT9}} (mentioned; concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCTT11}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCTT13}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCYT6}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCYT7}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCYT8}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCYT9}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCYT10}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCYT11}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCTT4}} (voice; concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TDBPCYT12}} (concurrent events) }} |} ==Trivia== *In the comics, J. Jonah Jameson is an editor-in-chief at ''[[The Daily Bugle]]'' newspaper, the boss of [[Peter Parker]], and [[Spider-Man]]'s harshest critic. *To offer a better reflection of the changes in the state of journalism, the mannerisms of the [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]] version of Jameson mimic those of the American far-right conspiracy theorist {{WPS|Alex Jones}} and the setup of ''[[The Daily Bugle]]'' is similar to Jones' online podcast and news network ''{{WPS|InfoWars}}''.[https://variety.com/2019/film/news/spider-man-blip-simmons-marvel-gyllenhaal-1203265915/ ‘Spider-Man' Writers Break Down What Really Happened During the Marvel Blip] ==Behind the Scenes== *J. Jonah Jameson in the [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]] is the third live-action incarnation of the character, following an incarnation portrayed by {{WPS|David White (actor)|David White}} & {{WPS|Robert F. Simon}} and another incarnation that was also portrayed by [[J.K. Simmons]] in [[Sam Raimi]]'s {{WPS|Spider-Man in film#Sam Raimi films (2002–2007)|''Spider-Man'' trilogy}} from [[Sony Pictures Entertainment]]. *J.K. Simmons first expressed interest in reprising his role as J. Jonah Jameson in the Marvel Cinematic Universe back when ''[[Spider-Man: Homecoming]]'' was about to start filming, but Jameson was not included in that film.[https://www.etonline.com/news/183317_exclusive_jk_simmons_reacts_to_spider_man_petition_calling_for_his_return EXCLUSIVE: J.K. Simmons Reacts to 'Spider-Man' Petition Calling for His Return: 'I'll Never Close the Door on] However, according to [[Kevin Feige]] and [[Amy Pascal]], it was always part of the plan to bring Simmons back to play Jameson.[https://comicbook.com/marvel/2019/07/07/spider-man-far-from-home-jk-simmons-jonah-jameson-cameo-actor/ How Spider-Man: Far From Home Pulled Off That Post-Credits Cameo] *J. Jonah Jameson was brought into the story of ''[[Spider-Man: Far From Home]]'' once it was decided to use the news to let [[Mysterio]] expose [[Spider-Man]]'s true identity. According to [[Jon Watts]], had J.K. Simmons refused to reprise the role, they would have scrapped Jameson's presence and replaced him with another character.[https://www.insider.com/spider-man-far-from-home-director-interview-on-biggest-twists-2019-7 'Spider-Man: Far From Home' director explains the 6 biggest twists in the movie] *According to J.K. Simmons, the filmmakers of ''Spider-Man: Far From Home'' decided to make J. Jonah Jameson bald in order to evolve him from his comic book version and make him more contemporary: ''"The only thing we didn't a hundred percent see eye to eye on, I think, was how much is this character going to be the character from, I think, the comic books and from the [[Sam Raimi]] original trilogy, and how much do we want to evolve it and to have it be more contemporary or more… you know. I was very attached to what I had done previously for a variety of reasons. So I guess the compromise ended up being no hair"''.[https://collider.com/jk-simmons-j-jonah-jameson-spider-man-far-from-home-explained/ J.K. Simmons' ‘Spider-Man: Far from Home' Cameo Led to Some Creative Disputes] *[[Courtland Fuller]] was a stunt double for [[J.K. Simmons]] in the role of J. Jonah Jameson. *This version of the character is featured in the post-credit scene of ''{{WPS|Venom: Let There Be Carnage}}'' which ties into the ending of ''[[Spider-Man: Far From Home]]''. ==References== {{Gallery|J. Jonah Jameson/Gallery}} {{Wq|J. Jonah Jameson}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel|J. Jonah Jameson}} *{{WP|J. Jonah Jameson (film character)|J. Jonah Jameson}} [[es:John Jonah Jameson]] [[ru:Джей Джона Джеймсон]] [[zh:J・乔纳・詹姆逊]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man: Far From Home Characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man: No Way Home Characters]] [[Category:Promotional Campaigns Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Brown Hair]] [[Category:Gray Hair]] [[Category:Bald Hair]] [[Category:Blue Eyes]] [[Category:Journalists]] [[Category:The Daily Bugle Employees]]
